text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Protocol-Specified On-Trial Assessment — Combination Targeted Therapy (MET Inhibitor + EGFR TKI)

---

ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [Attending Oncologist]
DICTATED BY: [Fellow/Resident]
SERVICE: Thoracic Oncology / Phase I Program

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Ms. Margaret Yuen is a 64-year-old Chinese-American woman with metastatic EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma presenting for her protocol-specified on-trial assessment, approximately 4 months into enrollment on the tepotinib plus osi Phase I/II study.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Yuen has a well-known oncologic history beginning approximately 2 years ago when she presented with a left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma, Stage IIIB (T3N3M0), found to harbor an EGFR L858R exon 21 point mutation on both tissue NGS and liquid biopsy. Co-mutations noted at diagnosis included STK11 Q37*, KEAP1 G480V, and TP53 R248W. She was treated definitively with concurrent carbo/pacli chemoradiation to 60 Gy in 30 fractions, followed by consolidation/maintenance Tagrisso (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily per LAURA trial data. She had a good partial response lasting approximately 14 months on osi before developing systemic and intracranial progression.

At the time of progression, rebiopsy of a new right lower lobe nodule confirmed retained L858R with newly acquired T790M resistance mutation, and repeat NGS demonstrated high-level MET amplification (copy number ratio 5.8, up from 2.4 at diagnosis) as a co-resistance bypass pathway. Brain MRI showed two new metastases — a 1.4 cm right frontal lesion and a 0.7 cm left cerebellar lesion — both treated with single-session SRS without incident. Following SRS, she completed 6 cycles of Paraplatin/Alimta (Alimta) with modest systemic response and disease stabilization, followed by a brief bridge of single-agent Alimta maintenance while awaiting trial eligibility confirmation.

She enrolled on the current Phase I/II open-label trial of tepotinib 500 mg orally daily plus Tagrisso 80 mg orally daily approximately 4 months ago, specifically targeting the dual resistance mechanisms of T790M (addressed by Tagrisso) and MET amplification (addressed by tepotinib). First on-trial restaging at 8 weeks confirmed a partial response with the left upper lobe lesion decreasing from 1.8 x 1.4 cm to 1.2 x 0.9 cm, and resolution of the right lower lobe nodule and right adrenal nodule.

Today she presents for her next protocol-specified assessment. Overall she is feeling better than she has in several months. She reports noticeably improved energy and has been more active — she has resumed daily walks around her neighborhood and has been able to do some light gardening, which she had given up during the carbo/pem phase. She describes her general status as "much better than last year."

Current symptom review regarding known trial-related toxicities:
- Peripheral edema: Bilateral ankle and lower leg pitting edema, unchanged from prior visit, Grade 1 in severity. She has been diligent about wearing compression stockings and restricting dietary sodium. Not causing significant functional limitation.
- Diarrhea: Occasional loose stools, roughly 1-2 per day most days, occasionally up to 3. Consistent with Grade 1. She manages with dietary modification (avoidance of high-fat foods, lactose). Does not routinely require loperamide at this point.
- Skin: Persistent mild acneiform rash on face, upper chest, and upper back. Using the moisturizing regimen and topical clindamycin gel as previously prescribed. Rash is not painful and she is not distressed by it cosmetically.

She denies shortness of breath, cough, hemoptysis, chest pain, or pleuritic symptoms. No headache, visual changes, focal weakness, gait unsteadiness, or other neurological symptoms. She does not report any new bone pain or lymphadenopathy. Appetite is good; she has regained approximately 3 lbs since last visit after losing weight during the platinum-based chemotherapy phase. No fever, chills, or night sweats. No lower extremity pain or swelling beyond the bilateral ankle edema noted above.

She remains a retired schoolteacher. She lives with her husband who is supportive and accompanies her to visits. She is a former smoker (15 pack-years, quit >10 years ago). She does not drink alcohol. She has been compliant with trial drug administration and has a medication diary; she reports no missed doses in the last 4 weeks.

Current medications (selected):
- Tepotinib 500 mg PO daily (trial drug)
- Tagrisso (Tagrisso) 80 mg PO daily (trial drug)
- Folic acid 400 mcg PO daily (retained from Alimta era, continued per PI preference)
- Omeprazole 20 mg PO daily
- Ondansetron 8 mg PO PRN nausea (using rarely, approximately once/week)
- Topical clindamycin 1% gel to face/chest/back BID (for acneiform rash)
- Loperamide 2 mg PO PRN (using 1-2x/week)
- Compression stockings bilateral lower extremity, 20-30 mmHg

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Positive as above (Grade 1 edema, Grade 1 diarrhea, Grade 1 acneiform rash, mild fatigue — improved from prior).
Negative: dyspnea, hemoptysis, cough, chest pain, palpitations, headache, diplopia, hearing changes, dysphagia, nausea (at rest), vomiting, constipation, hematochezia, dysuria, bone pain, new skin lesions (other than rash), joint pain, easy bruising/bleeding.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
General: Well-appearing, pleasant woman in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Appears comfortable and in good spirits today.
Vitals: BP 118/72, HR 72, RR 16, Temp 36.8°C, SpO2 97% on room air, Weight [kg recorded in chart, ~3 lbs above last visit]
ECOG Performance Status: 1
Skin: Grade 1 papulopustular/acneiform rash involving bilateral cheeks, forehead, upper chest, and upper back. No frank pustules. No skin breakdown or superinfection. No palmar-plantar erythema.
HEENT: Conjunctivae clear, no icterus. Oral mucosa moist without mucositis or thrush.
Neck: Supple. No palpable cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Breath sounds diminished at the left apex with stable coarse crackles in the left upper lobe, consistent with known post-radiation fibrotic changes. No wheeze, no egophony, no signs of consolidation elsewhere. No rub.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. No JVD.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly. No palpable mass.
Extremities: Bilateral pitting edema to the mid-shin, 1+ in severity, symmetric. Stable from prior visit. No erythema or warmth to suggest DVT. Peripheral pulses intact.
Neurological: Alert and oriented. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Strength 5/5 bilateral upper and lower extremities. No cerebellar dysmetria. Gait steady and normal-paced in hallway. No Romberg. No focal deficits.
Lymph nodes: No axillary or inguinal adenopathy palpable.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (drawn today, reviewed in clinic)
CBC: WBC 6.2 x10^9/L, Hgb 11.6 g/dL (stable mild anemia, up from 9.1 nadir during platinum chemo), Plt 187 x10^9/L
Comprehensive metabolic panel: Na 138, K 4.0, Cr 0.9 mg/dL (CrCl est. 76 mL/min, stable), BUN 14, AST 22, ALT 28, Alk Phos 78, T. Bili 0.6, Albumin 3.9
Magnesium: 0.8 mmol/L (low-normal, continue oral supplementation)
ECG: QTc 432 ms (stable, within acceptable range per protocol; no significant change from baseline)
CEA: 8 ng/mL — down from peak of approximately 42 ng/mL at time of osi progression; trending toward normal (lab ULN 3.4 ng/mL). Notable improvement from prior visit value of approximately 14 ng/mL.
LFTs: within normal limits; no hepatotoxicity signal

---

IMAGING REVIEW
Most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (approximately 8 weeks ago, first on-trial restaging): confirmed partial response with the left upper lobe lesion at 1.2 x 0.9 cm, resolution of right lower lobe nodule and right adrenal nodule. No new lesions identified. Next restaging CT is scheduled per protocol in approximately 6 weeks.

Most recent brain MRI (6-month SRS follow-up, approximately 2 months ago): Right frontal SRS site showed continued treatment evolution/post-treatment changes, minimal residual rim enhancement at 0.5 cm, consistent with radiation necrosis. Left cerebellar SRS site: complete radiographic response, no longer visible. No new intracranial lesions. No leptomeningeal disease.

No new imaging available today; per protocol, next imaging due in approximately 6 weeks.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. METASTATIC EGFR L858R-MUTANT NSCLC, ADENOCARCINOMA, WITH MET AMPLIFICATION AND ACQUIRED T790M RESISTANCE — currently on clinical trial combination targeted therapy (tepotinib + osi), with confirmed partial response on most recent imaging.
   - The patient continues to show a meaningful response to the tepotinib/osi combination. CEA has decreased substantially and is continuing to trend down toward normal. She is clinically improved with better functional status and energy. This is an encouraging 4-month update.
   - Continue tepotinib 500 mg PO daily and osi 80 mg PO daily per protocol. No dose modifications indicated at this time.
   - Next on-trial restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis scheduled in approximately 6 weeks per protocol.
   - Annual brain MRI is also coming up; will coordinate with next restaging window to minimize imaging burden.
   - Trial-related labs (CBC, CMP, ECG) to be repeated in 3 weeks per protocol schedule.

2. GRADE 1 PERIPHERAL EDEMA (tepotinib-related)
   - Stable. Consistent with known MET inhibitor class effect. Managed conservatively with compression stockings and sodium restriction.
   - No diuresis needed at this time. Weight stable. Will reassess at next visit; if progresses to Grade 2, will discuss fluid management strategies with the PI.

3. GRADE 1 DIARRHEA (Tagrisso-related)
   - Stable and manageable. Dietary modifications in place. PRN loperamide available.
   - No dose modification warranted.

4. GRADE 1 ACNEIFORM RASH (EGFR TKI class effect)
   - Ongoing, stable, Grade 1. Topical clindamycin gel and moisturizing regimen in place.
   - No systemic antibiotics needed at present. Patient is not significantly distressed by this. Continue current management.

5. MILD ANEMIA (residual from prior platinum chemotherapy)
   - Hgb 11.6 g/dL, improved from nadir. Not requiring intervention at this time. Monitor on routine CBCs.

6. LOW-NORMAL MAGNESIUM
   - Continue oral magnesium supplementation. Recheck with next labs.

7. INTRACRANIAL DISEASE — STABLE POST-SRS
   - Excellent intracranial control per last brain MRI. Right frontal site evolving as post-treatment change. Left cerebellar site in complete radiographic response. No new CNS metastases. No new neurological symptoms.
   - No neurosurgical or radiation oncology intervention needed at this time. Continued surveillance per protocol.

8. INCIDENTAL RIGHT PARIETAL EXTRA-AXIAL LESION (likely meningioma, 4 mm)
   - Stable on serial imaging. No intervention. Will continue to monitor on routine brain MRI.

9. GENERAL SUPPORTIVE CARE AND WELLBEING
   - Patient is feeling significantly better than during prior systemic chemotherapy. Engaged in light physical activity. Encourage continued walking and activity as tolerated.
   - Nutritional status improved; weight trending up appropriately.
   - Supportive care medications: omeprazole, PRN ondansetron, PRN loperamide. No changes.
   - Discussed continued importance of trial drug compliance; patient is adherent per diary review.
   - Social work contact available if needed; patient's husband is engaged and supportive.

Return to clinic in 3 weeks for protocol-mandated labs and interim toxicity assessment; full restaging CT and on-trial assessment in approximately 6 weeks.

Time spent in counseling and coordination: approximately 35 minutes.

[Electronically signed — Attending Physician, Thoracic Oncology / Phase I Program]
